title: 1.4 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lesson 1.4!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which best describes *collective learning* as a concept?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the unique human ability to share and collect information and knowledge as a group
the forms of language that humans use to communicate complex, abstract ideas
the ability the internet has given us to connect and learn from people across the globe
the human tendency to learn better in groups than individually

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which best describes how collective learning aided humans at the beginning of our history? Choose the *_best_* answer.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It helped people share important information about how to survive.
It pushed people to connect with one another to form religious communities.
It enabled people to use written language to transmit knowledge.
It taught people to cooperate and form states and empires.

assessment_items.question_content:
*The Industrial Revolution was not started by one singular invention but rather an accumulation of innovations and ideas that took place over time, leading to advancements in manufacturing and production. 

**Which historical concept does this example illustrate?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: collective learning
symbolic language
cause and effect
change and continuity

assessment_items.question_content:
**In foraging societies, elders taught younger generations what they knew about survival, such as how to hunt and which plants to gather. Which historical concept does this illustrate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: collective learning
cultural exchange
usable history
scale-switching

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does symbolic language help humans learn and build knowledge together over time?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It allows us to share and build on complex ideas that aren’t right in front of us.
It helps us connect with others and form strong communities across large regions.
It lets us write things down and share them with lots of people.
It doesn’t really affect how we learn, just how we communicate.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What defining attribute of *Homo sapiens* distinguishes humans from all other life forms, including earlier hominids?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the capacity for complex symbolic thought and language
the ability to walk upright on two legs
the use of basic tools for hunting and gathering
a diet that includes both plants and animals

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following illustrates how symbolic language differs from spoken language?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Gestures, such as a handshake, can communicate meaning without words.
Images and art can express ideas without using speech.
Spoken words are the only way people can share complex ideas.
Symbolic language helps us express abstract ideas clearly through speech.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following gestures is an example of symbolic language?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: thumbs up to indicate approval
scratching your head because of an itch
typing numbers to calculate a sum
a person eating breakfast

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is one example of collective learning having a *negative* impact on human interaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: institutionalization of racism
spread of viruses
loss of symbolic language
development of networks

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following classroom scenarios best represents collective learning?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a classroom debate over the ethics of dropping the atomic bombs to end World War II
student’s independently outlining information from the textbook
teacher-led lecture about globalization
a class quietly viewing a documentary on the history of genocides around the world

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which modern development best demonstrates the power of collective learning through global sharing of information and ideas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: using the internet to access online discussion forums
the popularity of Harry Potter books among young adults
using AI to explore ideas and ask better questions for your own understanding
watching tutorial videos to learn a new skill

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best illustrates how the internet demonstrates collective learning and contributes to human progress?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The internet allows people to share, access, and build upon knowledge at a global scale.
The internet allows individuals to learn in an isolated environment.
Online forums and social media provide a platform for the spread of misinformation.
Digital communication does not require face-to-face interactions.
